process_name: Lepton-flavor-violating neutral-charm decay D0 -> e mu
implementation_difficulty_reason: Missing implementation needs a new Delta C = 1 charged-LFV four-fermion operator convention and D0 -> e mu branching-ratio normalization; the existing Delta F = 2 meson-mixing basis and mu->e gamma dipole checker do not cover it.

commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "D0.*e.?mu|D\\^0.*e.?mu|D0.*mu.?e|D\\^0.*mu.?e|c.?->.?u.*e.*mu|c.?->.?u.*mu.*e|D0.?->.?e|D0.?->.?mu" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Focused C006 search returned no D0 -> e mu or c -> u e mu implementation in the required implementation/test directories; exact hits were D0-mixing mu_had_GeV false positives.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252 and quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941 are D0 mixing, not D0 -> e mu.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 and quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:668 are the neutral-meson D0 mixing policy/evaluator, not rare LFV charm.
flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 and scanParams/scan.py:524 implement/call the separate mu -> e gamma dipole constraint.

status_history.reason: Initial PKA source extraction and C006 draft deposit.
Writer polish completed for wa_w5b_charm_edm; numerical claims checked against sidecar/source snapshots.
Checker verified CHK-1 through CHK-8 for ca_w5b_charm_edm.
Opus round 2 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_002_index.md.

status_history.notes: PDG, LHCb, Belle, and BABAR D0 -> e mu limits verified.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:}
\(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to e^\pm\mu^\mp)\).
This entry covers the charged-lepton-flavor-violating neutral-charm decay
\(D^0\to e^\pm\mu^\mp\).  It is a \(\Delta C=1\), \(c\to u e\mu\)
null test with charge-conjugate final states included in the experimental
limit.  In flavor structure it is an up-sector analogue of semileptonic LFV
probes such as \(B^+\to K^+ e^\pm\mu^\mp\), but with a purely leptonic
two-body \(D^0\) final state.
sections.pdg_prose:
The sidecar source \texttt{PDG2026:D0EMu}, PDG Live/API listing S032.40
accessed on 2026-05-16, gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(D^0\to e^\pm\mu^\mp) < 1.3\times 10^{-8}
  \qquad (90\%~{\rm C.L.}).
\]
The PDG-used input is the 2016 LHCb search
\texttt{LHCb2016:D0EMu}, based on \(3.0\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\) of \(pp\) data
at \(\sqrt{s}=7\) and \(8\,{\rm TeV}\) and normalized with
\(D^0\to K^-\pi^+\).  The same LHCb source reports no significant excess.
Earlier post-2008 B-factory limits were weaker:
\texttt{Belle2010:D0EMu} reported
\(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to e^+\mu^-)+\mathcal{B}(D^0\to\mu^+e^-)<2.6\times10^{-7}\),
and \texttt{BaBar2012:D0EeMuMuEMu} reported
\(\mathcal{B}(D^0\to e\mu)<3.3\times10^{-7}\), both at 90\% C.L.  The
local snapshots are
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/C006/pdg2026\_d0\_emu\_pdgLive.txt},
\texttt{lhcb2016\_arxiv1512\_00322.txt},
\texttt{belle2010\_arxiv1003\_2345.txt}, and
\texttt{babar2012\_arxiv1206\_5419.txt}.
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped flavor already generates nonuniversal \(c\)- and \(u\)-quark
couplings through localization and KK exchange.  C006 additionally requires
lepton-flavor violation, so it is not a quark-only \(\Delta F=2\) constraint.
In a lepton-extended RS pipeline it would constrain four-fermion
\((\bar u\Gamma c)(\bar e\Gamma\mu)\) structures, flavor-changing \(Z\)-like
couplings combined with lepton flavor violation, scalar exchange, or explicit
leptoquark/R-parity-violating UV completions.  It is therefore a roadmap
process for joint up-sector and charged-lepton flavor alignment.
sections.post_2008:
The arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor} predates the modern
\(D^0\to e\mu\) search sequence.  Belle's 2010 result
\texttt{Belle2010:D0EMu} set the combined charge-mode limit at
\(2.6\times10^{-7}\), BaBar's 2012 continuum-charm search
\texttt{BaBar2012:D0EeMuMuEMu} gave \(3.3\times10^{-7}\), and LHCb's 2016
hadron-collider analysis \texttt{LHCb2016:D0EMu} became the current
PDG-used input at \(1.3\times10^{-8}\).  The LHCb source describes this as
an order-of-magnitude improvement over the previous limit and notes
sensitivity to leptoquark and R-parity-violating SUSY parameter space.
sections.validity:
The experimental upper limit is a clean LFV null result.  The RS interpretation
is model dependent: the quark-only scan has no charged-LFV \(c\to u e\mu\)
operator, and different chiral structures map differently onto the two-body
\(D^0\) rate.  A conservative future use should compare the limit to a defined
\(\Delta C=1\) LFV Wilson basis, with the \(D^0\) decay constant and lepton
mass effects stated explicitly, rather than reusing the neutral-meson mixing
basis.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The sidecar \texttt{code\_coverage} block records the required
greps across \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no
\(D^0\to e^\pm\mu^\mp\), \(c\to u e\mu\), or rare-charm LFV implementation.
Nearby hits are different observables: \(D^0\) mixing is configured at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py}:252 and evaluated at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py}:941; the modern policy surface lists
only neutral \(D^0\) mixing at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py}:23 and evaluates that
bridge at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py}:668.  The
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) dipole checker at
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py}:75, called from
\texttt{scanParams/scan.py}:524, is a separate charged-LFV observable.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{MEDIUM.}  A first live constraint would need a new \(\Delta C=1\)
LFV operator normalization and a \(D^0\to e\mu\) branching-ratio wrapper.  It
should not require a global fit or the existing \(\Delta F=2\) RG machinery,
but the current neutral-meson SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR basis is not sufficient.
sections.key_references:
Process-local keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{PDG2026:D0EMu}, \texttt{LHCb2016:D0EMu},
\texttt{Belle2010:D0EMu}, \texttt{BaBar2012:D0EeMuMuEMu}, and
\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008:CompositeFlavor}.
